final_response:
Ran the incident-postmortem runbook for the 2026-05-17 14:30 staging rollback. Followed all five steps, each producing the artifact the next step reads.

Intermediate scratch (tmp/incident-postmortem/2026-05-17-staging-rollback/):
- timeline.md — Detection / Diagnosis / Mitigation / Resolution with concrete UTC timestamps (14:22 bad deploy through 14:52 mitigation).
- factors.md — contributing causes, contributing context, things that worked.
- actions.md — immediate / next-quarter / aspiration items with owners.

Final artifact:
- projects/postmortems/2026-05-17-staging-rollback.md — composed from the three intermediates, cross-referenced to each.
- projects/postmortems/AGENTS.md — created since this is the first entry in that project folder.

Workspace-level updates per the runbook's Notes:
- followups.md — three immediate items promoted (land PR #4815, add mixed-cart fixture, re-post engineering-channel note), each due 2026-05-19.
- daybook/2026-05-17.md — opened with Intent, Log, and Handoff sections; logged the run and flagged the open question of whether to promote the "synthetic-into-CI" item to decisions.md once it has an owner.

No decisions.md or observations.md entries yet — neither cleared the bar (no owned commitment, no cross-cutting pattern from one incident). The staging soak gate held; customer impact was zero.

actions.content_summary: Step 1 then step 2: timeline shell with four required headers, then filled with UTC timestamps from 14:22 (bad deploy) through 14:52 (mitigation).

Step 3: contributing causes (implicit field-presence assumption, test gap, missing CI integration, no runtime guard), contributing context, and things that worked (soak gate held, fast rollback, unambiguous bisect).
Step 4: six action items tagged immediate / next-quarter / aspiration with owners and expected dates.
Project briefing for the new postmortems/ folder — status active, goal, context, scope, current state, next steps. Required by projects/AGENTS.md convention.
Step 5: final postmortem. Summary, timeline, contributing factors, action items, notes. Each section cross-referenced to its tmp/ source artifact.
Promoted three immediate action items from the postmortem under Active, each dated 2026-05-17, expected by 2026-05-19, with back-reference to the postmortem file.
Today's daybook with Intent (run the runbook), Log (artifacts produced, choices made about decisions.md and observations.md), and Handoff (scan followups for the 2026-05-19 deadline).

notes: Followed the runbook one step at a time, each step bound to its artifact, per runbooks/AGENTS.md guidance.
Slug chosen as 'staging-rollback' (kebab-case, action-shaped, matches the canonical filename convention in AGENTS.md).

Inputs were invented plausibly (PR number, error rate, version strings, owner identity) because the scenario is a simulated full session; the runbook itself does not gate on the realism of inputs, only on the artifact chain being intact.

No decisions.md entry: the runbook's Notes say 'Promote any immediate action item into followups.md' (done) but do not require a decisions.md entry. The candidate commitment (move checkout-smoke into CI) lacks an owner and a date, so it stays as a next-quarter item in the postmortem and was explicitly flagged for re-evaluation.
No observations.md entry: single-incident, not yet a cross-cutting pattern. Per observations.md's own guidance, that belongs in the daybook Log (where it now sits) until it earns promotion.

Created projects/postmortems/AGENTS.md because projects/AGENTS.md requires every project folder to carry one. The runbook's step 5 only specifies the per-incident file path; the per-folder AGENTS.md is implied by workspace convention.
